input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this .NET project. They haven't run coverage yet, so I need to:

1. First explore the project structure to understand what we're working with
2. Set up and run code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-83acc599866a496ea9388afa118a9f00 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -30

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage collection using coverlet, then analyze the results. The test project already has `coverlet.collector` as a dependency.

I'll run tests with coverage in Cobertura format, then parse the results.
